content.headline: At 63, I Finally Found the One Yard Tool I Can Use Without Spending Three Days on the Couch Afterward
content.subheadline: One retired teacher's honest story — and the heartbreaking reason the founder is selling his entire remaining inventory at 55% off

sections.body_html:
At 63, I finally found the one yard tool I can use without spending the next three days on the couch with a heating pad on my lower back.

I know how that sounds. But after eight years of dreading October — eight years of leaves piling up while my old gas blower sat in the garage like a dead anchor — I’m not exaggerating even a little.

And after what the founder just shared about why he’s clearing his entire inventory right now, I couldn’t stay quiet anymore.

visual_placeholder.description: A relaxed woman in her early 60s standing on an autumn leaf-covered wooden deck, holding a compact handheld blower with one hand, arm loose and natural at her side, smiling with quiet satisfaction. Golden late-afternoon light, warm and domestic atmosphere.

sections.heading: The October Morning She Called Her Son — And Hated Herself For It
sections.body_html:
Dorothy Simmons, 63, is a retired fourth-grade teacher from Columbus, Ohio. She raised three kids in a house with a big backyard and a long driveway lined with oak trees. For most of her adult life, she took pride in maintaining all of it herself.

"I was always the one who did the yard," she says. "My husband traveled for work. I never needed help."

But two autumns ago, something changed.

She dragged her gas blower out of the garage — a heavy orange machine she’d owned for twelve years. She set it on the driveway, checked the fuel, and pulled the starter cord.

Nothing.

She pulled again. And again. On the eighth pull, a sharp twinge in her lower back made her stop breathing for a second.

She went inside, sat at the kitchen table, and did something she had never done before in twenty-two years of homeownership.

She called her son to come do the leaves.

"I felt embarrassed," she admits quietly. "I’m not old. I’m healthy. But I felt old that morning. And I really, really didn’t want to feel that way."

If that story sounds familiar, keep reading. Because what Dorothy found next changed everything — and right now, you can get it for more than half off. There’s a reason for that, and it’s not a marketing trick.

visual_placeholder.description: A woman in her early 60s sitting at a kitchen table with a mug of coffee, looking slightly reflective, autumn leaves visible through the window behind her. Warm, realistic home interior. The expression is honest, not dramatic.

sections.heading: The Dirty Secret About Gas Blowers Nobody Talks About After 55
sections.body_html:
Here’s what nobody tells you when you buy a gas blower: it was engineered for a 35-year-old landscaper who uses it professionally, eight hours a day.

It was never designed for you.

A standard gas blower weighs between 8 and 12 pounds — before you fill the tank. The starter cord requires a sharp, sudden pull of 15 to 20 pounds of force. The vibration travels straight up your arm into your shoulder and neck. After 20 minutes, your back knows it happened.

And if it doesn’t start on the first pull? You pull again. And again. Until something gives — usually your patience, your rotator cuff, or both.

Warning: The repetitive yanking motion of a gas blower starter cord is one of the leading causes of rotator cuff strain in adults over 50 — an injury that often requires months of physical therapy and can permanently limit shoulder mobility.
47,000
Back & shoulder injuries from yard work annually (U.S.)
9.5 lbs
Average gas blower weight (empty tank)
$150+
Average annual gas blower maintenance cost
8 pulls
Average cord pulls to start a cold engine

And here’s the real question: does your blower actually leave the garage anymore?

Or does it sit there, month after month, because getting it out, fueling it, and starting it feels like too much effort for a 15-minute task?

Dorothy knows that feeling. "That blower cost me $280. By the end, I used it maybe twice a year. The rest of the time I’d just sweep. Or just leave the leaves there."

The problem was never her. The problem was the tool.

visual_placeholder.description: Split image: Left side shows a large dusty orange gas blower leaning against a cluttered garage wall, clearly unused. Right side shows a compact Seese Pro blower held easily in one open palm. Clean, honest, no exaggeration.

sections.heading: Then a Neighbor Sent Her a Text That Changed Everything
sections.body_html:
It was a Saturday morning in September. Dorothy’s neighbor Carol — 67, sharp as a tack — texted her a photo.

It showed a blower barely bigger than a hair dryer, sitting in the palm of one hand.

"I just did my whole back patio in four minutes," Carol wrote. "No cord. No gas. No back pain. You need this."

Dorothy’s first reaction was skepticism. "It looked like a toy. I thought, there’s no way something that small actually works."

What she didn’t know yet was this: the Seese Pro™ Ultra-Light Blower runs a brushless motor at 30,000 RPM — the same motor technology found in professional drones and high-performance electric vehicles. It delivers over 200 MPH of concentrated, directed airflow. In a body that weighs 950 grams.

That’s less than a 2-pound bag of sugar.

One button. No cord to pull. No gas to mix. No spark plugs to replace. You pick it up, press the button, and it works. Every single time.

RuntimeFuel-dependent, fumes, noise40 min per charge
Use casesOutdoor leaves onlyLeaves, car drying, frost, workshop
One-handed useNo — two hands requiredYes — designed for it

Dorothy ordered one that same afternoon.

"The first time I used it, I cried a little. I’m a bit embarrassed saying that. But I’d spent two years feeling like I couldn’t do my own yard anymore. And there I was. Doing it. By myself. In five minutes. Standing straight."

visual_placeholder.description: A senior woman's relaxed hand holding the Seese Pro blower effortlessly with one hand, autumn leaves swirling in the air in front of a wooden deck. The blower looks small, modern, and effortless to hold. Warm golden afternoon light.

sections.heading: What Happens When You Give People Back Their Independence
sections.body_html:
Dorothy isn’t alone. Thousands of homeowners over 55 have made the switch — and they all say something that goes beyond leaves and driveways.

It’s not just about the yard work. It’s about what the yard work represents.

“I’m 67 and I’ve had two back surgeries. My orthopedic surgeon told me years ago to stop using my gas blower or I’d end up back on the table. I found the Seese Pro and figured I had nothing to lose. I cleared my entire driveway — 200 feet of it — in under 10 minutes. Standing completely straight the whole time. Zero pain the next morning. I genuinely wish I’d found this five years ago.”
Robert M., 67, Phoenix, Arizona ★★★★★
“My husband bought this as a joke — he said, ‘That tiny little thing?’ Then he watched me clear the entire back patio in four minutes without stopping once. Now he’s stolen it for the garage, the workshop, and drying the car after washing it. We use it more than any other tool we own. It’s a little embarrassing how much we love it.”
Linda K., 61, Nashville, Tennessee ★★★★★

The 40-minute battery runs long enough to clear a large patio, dry an entire car without water spots, or defrost a windshield in under two minutes — no scraping, no frozen fingers.

And the brushless motor has no friction parts that wear down over time. Which is exactly why the Seese Pro comes with a lifetime replacement guarantee. Not 90 days. Not one year. Lifetime.

Good to know: The Seese Pro replaces four tools at once — your yard blower, your car drying cloths, your workshop dust blower, and your ice scraper. Most users say they’ve cleared an entire shelf in their garage within the first month.

visual_placeholder.description: 2x2 grid of warm lifestyle shots: top-left shows a senior man clearing a leaf-covered driveway upright and relaxed; top-right shows the blower drying a car's wet surface with droplets flying; bottom-left shows it clearing frost from a windshield; bottom-right shows it blowing sawdust in a workshop. All feel competent, warm, and real.

sections.heading: A Personal Note From Marcus Reid, Founder of Seese Pro™
sections.body_html:
My name is Marcus Reid. I built the Seese Pro three years ago.

I didn’t build it because I saw a gap in the market.

I built it because I watched my mother fall.

She was 68. It was a cold October morning in Portland. She was trying to start her gas blower on the driveway — the same one she’d owned for eleven years. On the eighth cord pull, the recoil threw her off balance. She hit the concrete hard. Two broken ribs. Four days in the hospital.

When I visited her, she looked at me from the hospital bed and said, "I just wanted to do my own driveway, Marcus. I just wanted to do it by myself."

I started designing what would become the Seese Pro the week she came home.

She was my first customer. The day her unit arrived, she called me from the driveway, voice shaking. "I did it. I did the whole thing myself. My back is fine. I’m fine."

She used that blower every single season. She was so proud of it — and honestly, so was I.

Last month, she passed away. She was 71.

I’ve been sitting with that for a few weeks now. And I’ve made a decision.

I’m liquidating my entire remaining warehouse inventory — 847 units — at 55% off the retail price.

My warehouse lease ends in three weeks. I’m not renewing it. I’m moving the business fully online. Rather than pay thousands in storage fees, I’d rather every remaining unit go directly to someone whose daily life it can actually improve.

That means $119.99 today instead of $249.99. And every order ships with three items included at no extra charge:

  • Rapid charger — full charge in under 90 minutes
  • Hard-shell carrying case — valued at $25, keeps it ready in any closet or car
  • Foam ear protection — because yes, 200 MPH is loud, and we’re not pretending otherwise

Combined bonus value: $110. Included free while inventory lasts.

Once these 847 units are gone, they’re gone. I won’t be restocking at this price. My mother would have wanted this inventory to reach as many people as possible. That’s the only reason I’m doing this.

— Marcus Reid, Founder, Seese Pro™

visual_placeholder.description: Flat lay, clean light grey background: the Seese Pro blower centered, surrounded by its rapid charger, open hard-shell carrying case, and foam ear plugs. Professional product photography, organized and inviting. No clutter.

sections.heading: Try It for 30 Days. If It Doesn’t Change Your Yard Work, Send It Back.
sections.body_html:
You have two choices right now.

You can go back to wrestling with the old gas blower. The eight cord pulls. The sore shoulder the next morning. The machine you keep meaning to use and never quite do.

Or you can try the Seese Pro for 30 days — completely risk-free.

If it doesn’t make your yard work faster, lighter, and easier than you thought possible at your age, send it back. Full refund. No questions, no hassle.

And if you keep it — lifetime replacement, guaranteed.

“I was nervous spending money on something new at my age. I’m retired, I’m careful. But the 30-day guarantee made me feel safe enough to try it. Now I tell every single person I know about this thing. My neighbor borrowed mine once and bought her own the next day. That’s the best review I can give.”
Beverly T., 69, Baton Rouge, Louisiana ★★★★★

Claim My Seese Pro at 55% Off →
